era: 1960s. Rock. E=5 -- the riff is everything. Reprise Records. National / PNW adjacent.

location.origin: National / PNW adjacent (Petaluma, California; Pacific Northwest ties)

theory_nodes.concept: "Spirit in the Sky" is one of the most recognizable guitar riffs in rock history. Four bars, D chord with chromatic walk-up, dense fuzz tone. Written in 15 minutes by Greenbaum (inspired by Porter Wagoner gospel TV). Billboard Hot 100 #3 (1970). Five decades of continuous sync licensing and cover royalties. Two UK #1 covers (1986, 2003). The riff is more famous than the artist's name.
theory_nodes.description: Spirit in the Sky as cultural saturation achievement.

theory_nodes.concept: Fuzz guitar in gospel context. Greenbaum's synthesis: dense fuzz tone (Maestro Fuzz-Tone / similar, 1969) + quasi-gospel lyrics. Fuzz was associated with psychedelia and hard rock; Greenbaum used it for spiritual warmth. Tambourine shimmer against low-mid fuzz = full-spectrum sound from minimal arrangement. Precedes Jesus Movement rock of early 1970s.
theory_nodes.description: Gospel fuzz synthesis: ahead of its cultural moment.

theory_nodes.concept: One-song catalog economics. Greenbaum wrote the song at ~27 years old; it has supported him for the rest of his life. Cover royalties from two UK #1 hits. Continuous sync licensing income (film, TV, commercial). Most-recognized aspect of output: the riff, more than the artist's name. The defining successful one-song catalog in rock.
theory_nodes.description: Perpetual royalty income from a single composition.

cross_references.relationship: E drops from 7 to 5. Stoner rock fuzz to gospel rock fuzz. Both use fuzz guitar toward opposite emotional ends: Wellwater heavy and cosmic; Greenbaum warm and spiritual.
Energy rises to 7. Hard Rock returns. Greenbaum E=5 is the valley between the heavy entries.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=5 vs E=8. Gospel rock warmth vs. howling ridge chaos. Human optimism (going to the place that's the best) against indifferent meteorological violence. E=5 music is smaller than the E=8 storm.

fuzz_guitar_history.greenbaum_context: 1969 recording uses fuzz for gospel warmth, not aggression -- unusual synthesis
